paragraphs.body: My bedroom when I was little was a mixed match and all over the place. The walls were painted a light lime green color, there was a wooden bed frame, pink bedding, and blue polka dot curtains. There was no clear color palette nor direction towards my room. I soon outgrew that room and decided to decorate it to my mature liking. Everything was to be chosen by me. My parents took me to Ikea to pick out things for my room. As we approached the blue and yellow IKEA building. We were overwhelmed by all the furniture and objects there were to choose from. Picking things out will probably take ages, and it did. For the most part I was indecisive about a lot of things to buy for my room, but what I did know was that I hated the overhead lighting in my bedroom. Whenever it was turned on, my room somehow magically transformed into a doctor's office. A doctor's office is the vibe I get from white tone lighting. Very unpleasant. Generally, I hated turning on overhead lighting, so the solution to that problem was to buy a lamp. My parents and I walked to the lighting section of the store, which had every lighting fixture turned on. It was the brightest section of the whole store. I quickly spotted a table lamp that had a simple design to my liking and, most importantly, cast a warm-toned light across the room.

annotations.comment: Nice job starting with narrative! Compelling voice and tone :)
annotations.context_left: IKEA building. We were overwhelmed by all the furniture and objects there were to choose

paragraphs.body: It looks like a lantern that you ignite with flame and let the heat wrap around you, but it's actually just an LED light bulb inside. It doesn't create a warm feeling when you're near, nor does it burn and hurt you when you touch it. It does warm up a room visually; the warmth that the lamp creates calms your eyes from the twelve-hour eye usage from the outside world. The warm atmosphere in the small room, where even the corner gets to feel the warmth, gives me comfort and makes me feel like a hot baked cookie relaxing in the oven and feeding on the warmth to recharge. The lamp blends right into my room with a black-colored base and a clear glass shield that protects the light bulb inside. Nothing out of the ordinary, but it's practical, which is what makes it more special in that case. The lamp also has a turn able knob on the side to adjust the dimness of the light. The knob gives me the ability to adjust it to the perfect dim lighting if necessary. In my eyes, this lamp beats every other lamp in the market. The lamp resides on the second shelf located on my desk, the perfect place for the lamp to shine its warm light. The perfect place for it to light up everything in my path. I never have to turn on the flickering white ceiling light in my room that gives a major doctor or working in an office job vibe, but I instead turn on my very practical lamp daily.

paragraphs.body: Well, the LED light bulb that goes with the lamp is actually energy-saving. Meaning it costs down on energy cost and consumption. Allowing the lamp to work efficiently while using less energy. The lamp allows me to see clearly, almost like the LED light bulb inside a lamp can be compared to my judgment; when the lightbulb is turned on, I am able to see the room, which allows me to make good decisions and sensible conclusions. When the lightbulb is off, I have blind insight and can’t seem to make a fair decision. There's also a time when the lightbulb starts to flicker because sometimes, I am indecisive. Judgment is not a biased decision, making a decision based on a preference. Judgment is not an opinion, as an opinion calls for a view or a belief. Judgment, however, is by taking reasoning and coming up with a conclusion with it. Judgment does come in both negative and positive forms because not all judgment is bad. Judgment can take many forms. Although being judgmental is a more negative attitude, with criticism attached to it. Know that judgment is not always nitpicking or highlighting the negative but can also be an acknowledgment of the strengths and the positive of a situation. So, I use my judgment in decision-making, and I make the more efficient choice.

annotations.text: Judgment is not an opinion, as an opinion calls for a view or a belief.
So, I use my judgment in decision-making, and I make the more efficient choice.

paragraphs.body: Since energy is the ability to do work. We need energy every day in our lives in order to function and be able to complete daily tasks. Whatever that be, thinking, reading, simply walking to class, or just the bathroom. Our bodies use energy for everything, even during sleep and when we breathe with our lungs. Every day we perform the act of work; we use our stamina and power to even wake up and decide to get out of bed. Since we exhaust ourselves quickly, it's very important that we use our energy wisely. We should pick and choose what we have energy for in our lives. Which is why we should conserve our energy when we use our judgment on deciding what to do and instead put more emphasis on our actions. Conserve our energy in order to put more toward action and less on decision-making. What is the point of making plans or deciding what needs to be done when at the end you get tired and never carry out those plans at all?

annotations.comment: You seem to have a second "word" here. How is "judgment" related to "energy"? Is it? What is the focus of this essay for you?
annotations.context_left: we breathe with our lungs. Every day we perform the act of work; we use our stamina and power

paragraphs.body: My brain uses up all my energy. All I do is overthink, overthink, and overthink. I spend every inch of my head over analyzing everything. All the possibilities and outcomes of every path that a choice opens. I will go over the past and the future. I exhaust myself rather than act and be able to work. Like the lamp, we have the ability to control the dimness of the light to be able to decide how much energy we want to use. So, our judgments shouldn't take a toll on us and should also take in less energy so more energy can go towards taking a step forward and allow us to physically perform.

annotations.text: My brain uses up all my energy. All I do is overthink, overthink, and overthink. I spend every inch of my head over analyzing everything. All the possibilities and outcomes of every path that a choice opens.
